channel.title: Martenweave Blog
channel.description: Practical notes on SAP migration, MDM, data governance, lineage, impact, and deterministic validation.

item.title: SAP MDG Manages Master Data. Who Manages the Implementation Knowledge?
item.description: SAP Master Data Governance can manage governed records, change requests, workflows, data quality rules and the operational lifecycle of master data.

item.title: Why SAP Migration Mapping Spreadsheets Break Down
item.description: Most SAP migration programmes begin with a mapping spreadsheet.

item.title: What to Prepare Before Starting an SAP MDG Implementation
item.description: An SAP MDG implementation often appears to begin with the platform.

item.title: How SAP Data Migration and MDG Implementation Should Work Together
item.description: SAP data migration and SAP Master Data Governance are often organised as separate workstreams.

item.title: Why an SAP MDG Implementation Needs an Independent Model Specification
item.description: An SAP MDG implementation contains several versions of the model.

item.title: SAP Data Migration Readiness Checklist
item.description: A migration team can complete mappings, build load files and pass several technical checks while the domain is still not ready for migration.

item.title: Why Mapping Completion Is Not Migration Readiness
item.description: A migration programme reports that 96% of mappings are complete.

item.title: How to Build a Single Source of Truth for SAP Mappings
item.description: Most SAP programmes claim to have a single source of truth for mappings.

item.title: How to Detect Dataset Gaps Before SAP Migration Testing
item.description: Many SAP migration programmes treat testing as the first serious encounter between the target model and the source data.

item.title: How to Perform Impact Analysis for an SAP Field Change
item.description: On Monday, a country team asks for one SAP field to become mandatory.

item.title: How to Trace a Legacy Field to an SAP Target Field
item.description: A defect appears during the second mock load.

item.title: How to Keep SAP MDG Design and Configuration Aligned
item.description: The design document says the field is optional.

item.title: How to Reduce Rework During an SAP MDG Implementation
item.description: The programme has already approved the model.

item.title: What an SAP MDG Handover Package Should Contain
item.description: The SAP MDG programme goes live on Friday.

item.title: Why Project Knowledge Disappears After Go-Live
item.description: Six months after go-live, an SAP MDG validation starts rejecting supplier changes.

item.title: How Martenweave Complements SAP MDG
item.description: A company has already invested in SAP Master Data Governance.

item.title: Martenweave vs Excel, Jira and Confluence
item.description: The programme already has an approved toolset.

item.title: Data Model Registry vs MDM Platform
item.description: A company has a data problem.

item.title: Data Model Registry vs Data Catalog
item.description: A migration manager searches the enterprise data catalogue for a customer field.

item.title: Why AI Should Propose Model Changes but Never Apply Them Directly
item.description: An AI agent reviews a migration workbook and finds an obvious inconsistency.

item.title: How Deterministic Validation Reduces Migration Risk
item.description: The migration workbook looks complete.

item.title: How to Manage Global and Local Requirements in SAP MDG
item.description: The global template says the field is mandatory.

item.title: How to Track SAP MDG Model Decisions
item.description: During a design workshop, the programme agrees that a supplier classification should be mandatory.

item.title: How to Validate That SAP MDG Configuration Matches the Approved Model
item.description: The design review is complete.

item.title: How to Build an Evidence-Based Migration Readiness Report
item.description: The steering committee sees a green dashboard.

item.title: How Better Model Traceability Reduces Rework
item.description: A migration defect appears during UAT.

item.title: How to Reduce Key-Person Risk in SAP Programmes
item.description: The programme has one person who knows how everything fits together.

item.title: How Much of an MDG Programme Happens Outside the MDG Platform?
item.description: The programme has selected SAP Master Data Governance.

item.title: How a Small Martenweave Pilot Can De-Risk a Large MDG Investment
item.description: The organisation is preparing a major SAP Master Data Governance programme.

item.title: How Metalhatscats Helps Teams Establish Model Governance
item.description: A programme has already created most of the artefacts it needs.

item.title: How to Turn Model Governance into an AMS Capability After Go-Live
item.description: The implementation programme has ended.

item.title: How to Use GitHub as a Change-Control Layer for SAP Data Models
item.description: A country team changes one row in a mapping workbook.

item.title: How to Govern Spreadsheet Changes Without Forcing Business Users into Git
item.description: The business owner receives a mapping workbook.

item.title: How to Design a Model Change Request That Business and Technical Teams Can Review Together
item.description: A business owner requests a change:

item.title: How to Prioritise Model Changes by Business Risk Instead of Ticket Urgency
item.description: A senior stakeholder opens a ticket:

item.title: How to Build a Model Risk Register for SAP Migration and MDG
item.description: The programme risk register looks healthy.

item.title: How to Turn Model Risks into an Actionable SAP Migration Backlog
item.description: The migration programme has identified a serious risk:

item.title: How to Define Exit Criteria for SAP Migration Model Risks
item.description: The programme has completed the remediation task.

item.title: How to Prove That an SAP Migration Risk Is Really Closed
item.description: The steering committee asks whether a critical migration risk has been closed.

item.title: How to Design a Closure Evidence Workspace for SAP Migration and MDG
item.description: The programme is preparing for a risk-closure meeting.

item.title: How to Connect Martenweave’s UI to Live Model, Dataset and Proposal Data
item.description: A migration architect opens Martenweave and searches for Customer Group .

item.title: How to Investigate an SAP Data Model Problem Before Changing the System
item.description: A migration test fails because Customer Group is missing.

item.title: How to Structure a Model Investigation Case So the Finding Can Be Reused
item.description: The German migration team spends three weeks investigating why Customer Group cannot be mapped reliably.

item.title: How to Build a Reusable Decision Library for SAP Migration and MDM
item.description: A programme has an approved decision:

item.title: How to Prevent Local SAP Decisions from Fragmenting the Global Data Model
item.description: A country team raises a reasonable request.

item.title: How to Decide Whether a Local SAP Requirement Belongs in the Global Model
item.description: A country team requests a new field:

item.title: How to Design Global and Local Model Inheritance Without Creating Configuration Chaos
item.description: The global supplier model defines Supplier Risk as a mandatory final classification for active strategic suppliers.

item.title: How to Resolve Conflicting Global and Local Rules Before They Reach SAP Configuration
item.description: The global design states:

item.title: How to Test Global and Local SAP Rules Against Real Migration Data
item.description: The global design says that Supplier Risk is mandatory for every active strategic supplier.

item.title: How to Turn Migration Rule Test Results into Model Decisions Instead of Defect Noise
item.description: The first migration-rule test produces 8,420 failures.

item.title: How to Build a Migration Finding Taxonomy That Business, Data and SAP Teams Use Consistently
item.description: The migration test reports that 4,860 supplier records failed the Tax Identifier rule.

item.title: How to Route Migration Findings to the Right Owner Without Creating Another Governance Bureaucracy
item.description: A migration-readiness run identifies 1,284 strategic suppliers without Supplier Risk.

item.title: How to Define Data Ownership That Still Works After the SAP Programme Ends
item.description: Three months after go-live, an AMS analyst receives an incident.

item.title: How to Build an AMS Model Change Process That Does Not Recreate the SAP Project
item.description: Six months after go-live, a country requests a new Supplier Classification value.

item.title: How to Separate SAP AMS Incidents, Service Requests and Model Changes Without Losing Traceability
item.description: A user reports that a supplier cannot be activated in SAP.

item.title: How to Detect When an SAP AMS Workaround Has Become an Unapproved Data Model
item.description: A supplier cannot be activated because its final risk classification is missing.

item.title: How to Retire SAP AMS Workarounds Without Breaking the Business Process
item.description: A temporary Supplier Risk workaround has been running for nine months.

item.title: How to Use AMS Incident Patterns to Improve the SAP Data Model Instead of Just Reducing Ticket Volume
item.description: The AMS dashboard shows progress.

item.title: How Martenweave Represents a Data Model in Markdown and YAML
item.description: A migration team needs to confirm the meaning of Customer Group .

item.title: Why Canonical Model Files Should Remain the Source of Truth
item.description: A business owner updates Customer Group in an Excel review file.

item.description: A migration analyst searches for:

item.title: How Deterministic Model Validation Works
item.description: A migration analyst adds a new mapping:

item.title: How Martenweave Represents Entities, Attributes, Rules and Relationships
item.description: A migration workbook contains one row:

item.title: How to Version a Data Model Without Turning It into Documentation Chaos
item.description: A Customer Group rule changes during an SAP programme.

item.title: How Model Diffing Should Work Across SAP Releases
item.description: An SAP programme compares its Wave 2 and Wave 3 model repositories.

item.title: How to Design Stable IDs for Enterprise Data Model Objects
item.description: A Customer Group attribute begins its life in one migration workbook.

item.title: Why Generated Indexes Should Be Disposable
item.description: A Martenweave user opens modelops.db and corrects the name of an Attribute directly in SQLite.

item.title: How to Package and Transfer a Martenweave Model Repository
item.description: An SAP migration programme reaches handover.

item.title: How to Build Field-Level Lineage for an SAP Migration
item.description: A migration programme says that it has lineage.

item.title: Why Table-Level Lineage Is Not Enough for Master Data
item.description: A migration architect opens the lineage view and sees:

item.description: A technical team proposes a simple change:

item.description: A team proposes three changes:

item.title: How to Detect Orphaned Fields, Rules and Mappings
item.description: A repository validates successfully.

item.title: How to Detect Broken Lineage After a Source or Target System Change
item.description: A source system is retired.

item.title: How to Model Source Authority and Fallback Paths Without Creating Ambiguous Lineage
item.description: A migration team traces Customer Group upstream and finds four possible sources:

item.title: How to Separate Design-Time Lineage from Runtime Evidence
item.description: A migration design states:

item.title: How to Use Lineage Evidence During Mock Loads, Cutover and Hypercare
item.description: Lineage is often treated as design documentation.

item.title: How to Build a Lineage-Based Migration Readiness Score Without Hiding Risk
item.description: A migration dashboard shows:

item.title: How to Explain Lineage and Impact to Business Owners Without Showing Them a Graph Database
item.description: A data architect opens a lineage viewer during a business review.

item.title: How Lineage Supports AMS Root-Cause Analysis After Go-Live
item.description: A production incident arrives in the AMS queue:

item.title: How to Connect Dataset Gaps to Lineage and Business Impact
item.description: A dataset-readiness report says:

item.title: How to Prioritize Migration Gaps by Business Risk Instead of Field Count
item.description: A readiness report identifies 137 data gaps.

item.title: How to Turn Migration Gap Findings into Reviewable Patch Proposals
item.description: A profiling script finds that the Product migration dataset has no Plant field.

item.title: Why AI Should Propose Model Changes but Never Approve Them
item.description: A Product migration dataset contains PLANNER GROUP , MRP TYPE and PROCUREMENT TYPE .

item.title: Why a Data Catalogue Is Not Enough for Migration Model Governance
item.description: A migration team is preparing Product data for SAP S/4HANA.

item.title: Why SAP MDG Is Not a Replacement for a Migration Model Registry
item.description: A programme is migrating Supplier bank data into SAP S/4HANA.

item.title: How Martenweave Should Work Alongside SAP MDG During a Migration
item.description: A programme is migrating Supplier bank data from several legacy systems into SAP S/4HANA.

item.title: Why Migration Mapping Workbooks Become Technical Debt—and What Should Replace Them
item.description: A migration programme creates a mapping workbook for Product planning data.

item.title: How We Convert a Mapping Workbook into a Canonical Model Without Losing Business Context
item.description: When we receive a migration mapping workbook, our first instinct should not be to convert every row into YAML.

item.title: How We Keep Business Decisions Traceable After the Mapping Workbook Is Gone
item.description: A migration team decides that MRP Controller must be derived differently for Plant PL30.

item.title: How We Prevent Temporary Migration Exceptions from Becoming Permanent Rules
item.description: During Mock Load 2, the Product planning migration has a coverage problem.

item.title: How We Model Data Quality Rules Without Mixing Business Policy and Technical Validation
item.description: A migration programme is preparing Supplier bank data for SAP.

item.title: How We Turn Validation Failures into Actionable Findings Instead of Another Error Report
item.description: We run a validation against a Supplier bank-data migration dataset.

item.title: How We Decide Whether a Finding Requires Data Correction, a Mapping Change or a Model Change
item.description: A Supplier bank-data validation fails for 3,400 records.

item.title: How We Prioritize Findings by Business Impact Instead of Error Count
item.description: We complete a Supplier bank-data validation run.

item.title: How We Prevent Duplicate Findings Across Mock Loads and Migration Waves
item.description: We run the Supplier bank-verification check during Mock Load 2.

item.title: How We Connect Findings to Owners Without Building Another Workflow Engine
item.description: We confirm a critical Supplier bank-data Finding.

item.title: How We Prove That a Finding Is Actually Resolved
item.description: We identify a critical Supplier bank-data Finding.

item.title: How We Detect When Evidence Has Become Stale
item.description: We resolve a critical Supplier bank-data Finding during Cutover Rehearsal 5.

item.title: How We Build a Migration Readiness View That Does Not Hide Exceptions
item.description: The Supplier migration dashboard shows:

item.title: How We Separate Technical Completeness from Business Readiness
item.description: A Supplier migration load finishes successfully.

item.title: How We Use Model Evidence During Cutover Approval
item.description: The Supplier migration team completes the final rehearsal.

item.title: Why a Successfully Loaded Material May Still Be Unusable
item.description: The material migration report looks successful.

item.title: How We Detect Missing Material Extensions Before Cutover
item.description: The material migration team reports:

item.title: Why Units of Measure Are One of the Most Dangerous Logistics Migration Objects
item.description: The unit-of-measure migration report looks harmless.

item.title: How We Model Packaging Hierarchies Without Losing Logistics Meaning
item.description: The material migration team reports that packaging data is complete.

item.title: Why S/4 Material Readiness Does Not Prove EWM Readiness
item.description: The material migration dashboard is green.

item.title: How We Create a Canonical Location Model Across ERP, EWM, TM and External Carriers
item.description: What happens when five systems agree that a location exists—but none of them agrees on what the location actually is?

item.title: How We Govern Lead Times Across Procurement, Production, Warehouse and Transportation
item.description: What exactly are we promising when we say the lead time is 12 days?

item.title: Why SAP Data Programmes Pay Consultants to Rediscover the Same Knowledge
item.description: Why are we paying senior consultants to answer the same question for the fourth time?

item.title: How a Canonical Data and Interface Registry Reduces Change-Impact Consulting Costs
item.description: Why can changing one master-data field require six weeks of analysis?

item.title: Why Mapping Workbooks Become a Recurring Consulting Cost
item.description: How many times should a transformation programme pay to map the same 1,400 legacy codes?

item.title: How Much Does a Green Dashboard Cost When Every Team Rebuilds the Evidence Behind It?
item.description: How much consulting effort sits behind one green status on a steering-committee slide?

item.title: Why Consultant Turnover Makes SAP Programmes Pay Twice for the Same Knowledge
item.description: What exactly leaves the programme when an experienced consultant leaves?

item.title: Where Martenweave Fits in Logistics: Managing the Data Behind Operations
item.description: Logistics problems are often described as operational problems.

item.title: Logistics Problems Rarely Start in Logistics
item.description: When a delivery fails, people usually look at the delivery.

item.title: SAP Logistics Data Migration Problems Usually Appear Too Late
item.description: Most SAP logistics migration problems are discovered at the worst possible moment.

item.title: What Is a Data Model Registry? A Practical Guide for Managers
item.description: Most companies do not lack documentation.

item.title: Why Data Migration Projects Need a Model Registry
item.description: Most migration programmes do not fail because nobody created a mapping.

item.title: From Mapping Spreadsheet to Governed Data Model
item.description: Most migration programmes begin with a spreadsheet.

item.title: Data Lineage vs Impact Analysis: Why Seeing the Flow Is Not Enough
item.description: Data lineage is one of the most attractive ideas in data management.

item.title: Data Quality Tools vs Model Validation: Clean Data Can Still Follow the Wrong Rules
item.description: A data-quality report can be completely green while the migration is still wrong.

item.title: Schema Registry vs Model Registry: Technical Compatibility Is Not Business Meaning
item.description: A message can be technically valid and still be wrong.

item.title: Why SAP Migration Teams Still Lose Control Even with the Right Tools
item.description: An SAP migration programme may have every major tool it is supposed to have.

item.title: Mapping Version Drift: Why SAP Migration Teams Keep Reopening the Same Decisions
item.description: Most migration teams believe they have a mapping problem only when a field is missing.

item.title: SAP Migration TCO: How Model Governance Reduces Rework and Project Cost
item.description: The licence fee is rarely the most dangerous part of migration cost.

item.title: Why Martenweave Creates More Value Across a Programme Than in a Single Project
item.description: A small migration project may not need Martenweave.

item.title: Why Data Governance Frameworks Stay on Paper During SAP Programmes
item.description: Many organisations already know what good data management should look like.

item.title: Why DAMA-Based Data Governance Fails to Reach SAP Migration Teams
item.description: Many companies have a data-governance framework.
